briefs.title: Burnaby Council Votes on Rezoning, Housing, and Infrastructure Contracts — March 24, 2026
briefs.summary: At its March 24, 2026 meeting, Burnaby City Council voted on several items affecting local businesses and development, including rezoning applications for mixed-use and self-storage projects, a $4.2M water metering contract, and a modular housing pilot project that was sent back to staff for further review. Several rezoning bylaws were advanced to first and second reading, meaning they are still moving through the approval process and have not yet become law.
briefs.action_required: If your business is near a rezoning site, monitor upcoming public hearings for your chance to comment.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Burnaby Council Approves Zoning Changes, Housing Plans, and New Bylaws at March 24 Meeting
briefs.summary: At its March 24, 2026 meeting, Burnaby City Council voted on several items affecting local businesses and development, including rezoning applications for mixed-use and self-storage projects, new parking and fee bylaws now in effect, and a modular housing pilot project sent back to staff for further review. Several zoning bylaw amendments were given first and second readings, meaning they are still moving through the approval process and have not yet become law.
briefs.action_required: If your business is near a rezoning site, monitor upcoming public hearings for your chance to comment.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Parking Meter and EV Charging Meter Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026 — final adoption
Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Amendment Bylaw No. 2, 2026 — final adoption
Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Amendment Bylaw No. 2, 2026 — final adoption

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Liquor Licence for Solo Karaoke on Kingsway Approved
briefs.summary: At the March 10, 2026 Burnaby Council meeting, Council voted to recommend that the provincial Liquor and Cannabis Regulation Branch (LCRB) — the body that issues liquor licences in BC — grant a liquor primary licence to Solo Karaoke at 102–6462 Kingsway. A liquor primary licence allows a business to serve alcohol as its main activity. The recommendation followed a staff investigation into previous allegations about the business, and the new owner appeared before Council to show the establishment would be a community asset.
briefs.action_required: If you operate a nearby business or have concerns, contact the BC LCRB directly — the final licence decision rests with them.
briefs.industries: Liquor

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby 2026 Sewer Parcel Tax Roll Confirmation
briefs.summary: Burnaby's Parcel Tax Roll Review Panel has voted to confirm and authenticate the 2026 Sewer Parcel Tax Roll — a list of properties and the sewer-related charges assigned to each. This decision is now set to go before full City Council for approval at the March 24, 2026 meeting.
briefs.action_required: Check your property's sewer parcel tax charge on the 2026 roll before Council approves it.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development

briefs.title: Burnaby Council approves contracts, housing plans, and rezoning applications at March 24 meeting
briefs.summary: At its March 24, 2026 meeting, Burnaby City Council voted on several items including a $4.2M water metering contract, hired equipment and asphalt paving contracts, a new housing authority strategic plan, and multiple rezoning applications for mixed-use and self-storage developments across the city. A modular four-plex housing pilot project was sent back to staff for further review.
briefs.action_required: If your business is near a rezoning site, watch for future public hearings where you can share your input.

key_dates.label: Burnaby REZ #24-23 Grand Promenade rezoning bylaw — First and Second Reading scheduled
Burnaby REZ #25-09 6535 Kingsway mixed-use rezoning — First and Second Reading scheduled
Burnaby REZ #25-21 2757 Norland Ave self-storage rezoning — First and Second Reading scheduled

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Council to vote on Burnaby Housing Authority's 2026–2030 Strategic Plan
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on approving the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation's (BHA) five-year Strategic Plan, which targets 1,000 new rental housing units by 2030. Approving this plan is also a required step before the City can release $2,000,000 in annual operating funding to the BHA for 2026.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on March 24 — this shapes rental housing supply in Burnaby through 2030.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — BHA 2026–2030 Strategic Plan approval
BHA financial statements & 2026 budget — Burnaby Council agenda
BHA first project — minimum 50 rental unit starts deadline

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Burnaby paying $36,250 in UBCM membership dues for 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on approving a $36,250.57 payment for the City's 2026 membership in the Union of BC Municipalities (UBCM) — the organization that gives local governments across BC a shared voice when dealing with the provincial and federal governments. The money is already set aside in Burnaby's 2026 operating budget, so no new spending would be required if Council approves.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision on March 24, 2026 — no direct action required for most business owners.
briefs.industries: Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to vote on $4.2M contract for Burnaby water metering technology
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a $4.2 million contract (including taxes) to Neptune Technology Group Canada Co. to supply and deliver water meters across the city. This is the first phase of Burnaby's Universal Water Metering Strategy — about 5,500 meters would go to meter-ready properties, and roughly 500 homes with secondary suites would also receive meters. For now, water bills would stay as flat annual charges, not usage-based.
briefs.action_required: Watch the March 24, 2026 council meeting — if you own property in Burnaby, your water billing setup may change.

key_dates.label: Burnaby water metering contract award council vote

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Technology

briefs.title: Council to vote on $2.6M modular four-plex housing pilot at 8315 14th Avenue, Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on awarding a $2,626,050 contract to Echelon Creative Inc. to build a modular (factory-built) four-unit residential building at 8315 14th Avenue as a pilot project. The City would own the building and rent out the units, with $350,000 in outside funding from FortisBC and the National Research Council reducing the City's share to about $2.28 million.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the March 24, 2026 council decision — it could signal more modular housing projects in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — modular four-plex contract award at 8315 14th Avenue

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to vote on awarding 19 hired equipment contracts worth $29.8M over three years
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding 19 separate contracts for hired equipment services — things like dump trucks, excavators, tree chippers, and hydro-vac units — to support city crews during busy periods and emergency callouts. The contracts would run from April 1, 2026 to March 31, 2029, with a total estimated value of $29,825,000 (about $9.9M per year), and could be extended for an additional two years if service and pricing hold up.
briefs.action_required: If you run an equipment or contracting business, watch for future RFSOs from Burnaby to bid on similar work.

key_dates.label: Burnaby hired equipment service contracts proposed start date
Burnaby Council vote on hired equipment contracts — public meeting

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics, Natural Resources

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.155M asphalt paving contract renewal with Blackrete Paving
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on renewing a contract with Blackrete Paving Ltd. for asphalt paving and concrete services for the City's Engineering Department. The proposed renewal covers April 1, 2026 to March 31, 2027, at an estimated cost of $1,155,000 including taxes, with final payment based on actual work completed.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the March 24, 2026 council decision if you work in paving or civil construction in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby asphalt paving contract renewal period starts — if approved
Burnaby asphalt paving contract renewal period ends

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Proposed: New Mixed-Use and Self-Storage Rezoning Applications Advance in Burnaby
briefs.summary: At its March 24, 2026 meeting, Burnaby Council voted to move several rezoning applications — meaning requests to change how land can be used — forward for further review. These include a 12-storey mixed-use building at 6535 Kingsway, a five-storey self-storage facility at 2757 Norland Avenue, and a large amended mixed-use tower project near Grand Promenade. Each rezoning bylaw still needs to pass additional readings and, in some cases, a public hearing before it becomes final.
briefs.action_required: If your business is near any of these sites, watch for upcoming public hearings where you can share your input.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Proposed: Four High-Rise Towers with 1,574 Homes Up for Approval in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on whether to allow city staff to move forward with a rezoning application — a formal request to change the rules for what can be built on a property — for 3856 and 3870 Carrigan Court in Burnaby. The proposal from developer Pinnacle International would replace a previously approved plan with four high-rise towers (up to 40 storeys) containing roughly 1,133 market strata units, 141 replacement rental units, 300 non-market affordable rental units, and ground-floor retail space.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the full rezoning report and future public hearing — this large development could affect nearby businesses and rental supply.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Carrigan Court rezoning enquiry authorization

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on major mixed-use tower expansion at Lougheed
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on whether to advance a rezoning application (REZ #24-23) that would allow developer Pinnacle International to expand its approved Lougheed-area project. The updated plan would add a 171-suite hotel, street-level and rooftop restaurants, and 70 more residential units — growing the two towers to 87 and 77 storeys — while reducing office space. Council must first approve moving the rezoning bylaw to its next formal reading stages before any changes can take effect.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — hotel, restaurant, and property businesses near Lougheed should track this decision.

key_dates.label: REZ #24-23 Burnaby rezoning bylaw — Final Adoption deadline

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Hotel & Accommodation, Food Service

briefs.title: Proposed: 12-Storey Mixed-Use Building at 6535 Kingsway, Burnaby — Council to Consider Rezoning
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on whether to advance a rezoning application for 6535 Kingsway to its first formal readings. The proposal, submitted by Intracorp, would allow a 12-storey building with 290 rental apartments and roughly 951 m² of ground-floor retail shops to replace the existing fast-food drive-through on the site.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council vote — this rezoning could change the retail and rental landscape along Kingsway.

key_dates.label: Burnaby REZ #25-09 — Council vote to advance rezoning to First and Second Reading

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Proposed: Five-Storey Self-Storage Facility at 2757 Norland Avenue Up for Approval
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on advancing a rezoning application (REZ #25-21) that would allow a five-storey self-storage building with about 1,008 units to be built at 2757 Norland Avenue. The proposed bylaw would change the property's zoning — the rules about what can be built there — from general industrial to a custom category that allows this type of storage facility.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council vote — this new self-storage facility could affect nearby businesses and industrial tenants.

key_dates.label: REZ #25-21 Burnaby rezoning bylaw — deadline for Final Adoption or application is cancelled

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Council to Consider Endorsing Burnaby's First Poverty Reduction Strategy
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on endorsing a new Poverty Reduction Strategy — a plan that outlines 84 recommended actions across areas like housing, employment, health, and cost of living. If endorsed, staff would work with community partners to build an implementation plan, and the Mayor would write to the provincial government asking for ongoing funding to help cities carry out local poverty reduction work.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on March 24, 2026 — it could shape future community programs and funding in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Poverty Reduction Strategy — Council endorsement vote

briefs.industries: Labour & Employment, Healthcare, Professional Services

briefs.title: Burnaby Consent Agenda: Building Permits, City Investments, and Burnaby 2050 Plan Updates
briefs.summary: At the March 24, 2026 Council meeting, four consent agenda items were approved for information. These covered building permit activity for January and February 2026, a year-end report on the city's 2025 investment program with a 2026 forecast, a status update on major civic construction projects, and an overview of how Burnaby plans to put its long-term community plan — Burnaby 2050 — into action.
briefs.action_required: Review the building permit activity reports if you are planning construction or development in Burnaby.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Finance & Insurance, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to consider: Burnaby building permit activity report for January–February 2026
briefs.summary: Council will consider receiving a report summarizing all building permits — official approvals that allow construction to begin — issued in Burnaby during January and February 2026. This is an informational item only; no new rules or fees are being proposed. The full permit data will be posted to the City of Burnaby website after the March 24, 2026 meeting.
briefs.action_required: Check the City of Burnaby website after March 24 for the published permit activity data.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to Consider Burnaby's 2025 Investment Results and 2026 Forecast
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive a report on how Burnaby managed its $2.08 billion investment portfolio in 2025, earning $106.63 million at a 4.43% return. The report also forecasts a lower return of around 3.20% (roughly $71 million) for 2026, reflecting falling interest rates and a more cautious investment approach due to economic uncertainty.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — lower investment returns may affect city budgets and future service funding.
briefs.industries: Finance & Insurance, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to Consider Status Update on Seven Major Burnaby Civic Construction Projects
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive a status update on seven large city-owned construction projects across Burnaby, including a new recreation centre at Burnaby Lake, a new RCMP building, and expanded community centres. No new spending decisions are being made — this is an information report only, confirming most projects are on schedule and on budget.
briefs.action_required: Monitor project timelines if your business is near any construction sites — traffic and access impacts are ongoing.

James Cowan Theatre handover to Parks, Recreation & Culture — Q1 2027

Brentwood Community Centre construction completion — Q1 2030

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Food Service, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan Implementation Action Plan Launch
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to receive a report on the proposed launch of an Implementation Action Plan (IAP) for the newly adopted Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan — a long-term blueprint that guides how the city grows and develops. The IAP would track progress, assign responsibilities, and report publicly on how well the city is meeting its planning goals, with a final plan expected to go before Council in early 2027.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the Burnaby 2050 webpage for updates — this plan will shape future zoning and development rules.

key_dates.label: Burnaby 2050 Implementation Action Plan — Council review expected

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Information Reports — March 24, 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to review a set of information reports at its March 24, 2026 meeting. No body text or specific report details were provided, so the exact topics under consideration are not yet known. Business owners should check the full agenda on the City of Burnaby's website for details that may affect their operations.
briefs.action_required: Check the City of Burnaby's website for the full agenda before the March 24, 2026 meeting.

key_dates.label: Burnaby City Council information reports review meeting date

briefs.industries: Professional Services

briefs.title: Burnaby Council Advances Zoning Changes and Adopts Parking & Fee Bylaws
briefs.summary: At its March 24, 2026 meeting, Burnaby Council gave first and second reading — meaning early-stage approval, not final sign-off — to three zoning amendment bylaws affecting properties on Lougheed Hwy, Springer Avenue, and Imperial Street. Council also gave final adoption to three bylaws updating parking meter rules, city fees and charges, and bylaw notice enforcement.
briefs.action_required: Check if your property or business is near 6800 Lougheed Hwy, 2209–2225 Springer Ave, or 5058 Imperial St — rezoning is in progress.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Proposed: Three Burnaby Zoning Bylaw Amendments Up for First and Second Reading
briefs.summary: At the March 24, 2026 meeting, Burnaby Council gave first and second reading — meaning an early approval step, not final passage — to three proposed zoning bylaw amendments affecting properties at 6800 Lougheed Hwy, 2209 & 2225 Springer Avenue, and 5058 Imperial Street. These bylaws must still pass further readings and receive final adoption before they become law, so no changes are in effect yet.
briefs.action_required: Monitor upcoming Council meetings for public hearings or final adoption votes on these three zoning bylaw amendments.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on 20-storey mixed-use rental tower at 5058 Imperial Street
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed zoning bylaw (Bylaw No. 14802) that would allow a 20-storey mixed-use building at 5058 Imperial Street, featuring market rental apartments and ground-floor commercial space. This proposal replaces a previously approved plan for a smaller six-storey rental building on the same site.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — this rezoning could affect nearby property owners, tenants, and commercial operators.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on rezoning and master plan update at 6800 Lougheed Hwy
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would update the master plan — a document that guides how a large site can be developed — for the Burnaby Lake Village area at 6800 Lougheed Hwy. The changes would revise the site layout, update building heights and massing (how big and bulky buildings can be), and add more open space, in line with Burnaby's new long-term planning rules (OCP 2050). No new buildings or lot splits are proposed at this stage.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — this could affect development plans or property values near 6800 Lougheed Hwy.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Proposed: 49-Storey Mixed-Use Tower with Retail at 2209 & 2225 Springer Avenue, Burnaby
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a zoning bylaw amendment that would allow a 49-storey residential tower — including a 9-storey stepped base (podium) with retail shops facing the street — to be built at 2209 and 2225 Springer Avenue in Burnaby. If approved, the rezoning would change what is legally permitted on this land to match the proposed mixed-use development designed by FaulknerBrowns Architects.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — approval could bring new retail space and foot traffic to the Springer Avenue area.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Burnaby Council adopts parking meter, fees, and bylaw enforcement amendments
briefs.summary: At its March 24, 2026 meeting, Burnaby City Council gave final adoption to three bylaws: an update to parking meter and EV charging meter rules, an amendment to the city's consolidated fees and charges, and a change to the bylaw notice enforcement rules. These changes are now in effect as of that meeting.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated Burnaby fees and charges schedule to check if any new rates apply to your business.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics, Retail

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to update EV charging meter rules and fee structures
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a bylaw amendment that would update the rules for public electric vehicle (EV) charging meters in the city. The proposed changes would expand the definition of EV charging meters to include faster chargers, allow fees to be charged by either time or energy used, and set a maximum time limit for how long a vehicle can occupy a public EV charging space.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the final adoption date — this could affect how you use or manage public EV charging spaces.
briefs.industries: Transportation & Logistics, Retail

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to update EV charging fees at public charging stations
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would update the fees charged at Burnaby's public electric vehicle (EV) charging stations. The new rates would charge drivers by the amount of energy used (per kWh) or by time, and would add an 'idle fee' — a per-minute charge for vehicles that stay plugged in after charging is done — to encourage faster turnover at chargers.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — if you use or manage EV charging at your business, review the new proposed rates.

key_dates.label: Burnaby EV charging fee bylaw — council adoption vote

briefs.industries: Transportation & Logistics, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Proposed: New Fine for Overstaying Time Limits at Burnaby Public EV Charging Spots
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a bylaw amendment that would add a new fine for drivers who exceed the maximum time limit at public electric vehicle (EV) charging meters in Burnaby. If approved, the penalty would be $75, reduced to $60 if paid early — giving drivers a financial reason to free up charging spots once their time is up.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — if you use or manage EV charging spaces, this fine could affect your customers or operations.
briefs.industries: Transportation & Logistics, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to consider correspondence items at March 24, 2026 Burnaby meeting
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to review a correspondence item requiring a decision at its March 24, 2026 meeting. Additional correspondence has been published in a separate public package for that date, but details on the specific item up for Council's decision are not provided in this agenda entry.
briefs.action_required: Check the full March 24, 2026 Council Correspondence package for details that may affect your business.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council correspondence decision — meeting date

briefs.industries: Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to Consider Petition Opposing 20-Storey Development at 5058 Imperial Street, Burnaby
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive a petition signed by 175 residents opposing a proposed 20-storey building at 5058 Imperial Street in Burnaby (rezoning application REZ #25-13). Residents say the development would block views, threaten retirement stability, and raise neighbourhood safety concerns — and they are asking Council not to move the rezoning forward at this time.
briefs.action_required: Attend the March 24, 2026 Council meeting to follow the discussion on REZ #25-13.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — REZ #25-13 petition considered

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Councillor Alison Gu announces she will not seek re-election in October
briefs.summary: At the March 24, 2026 Burnaby Council meeting, Councillor Alison Gu announced that she will not be running for re-election when her current term ends this October. She highlighted key achievements during her time on Council, including affordable housing initiatives, environmental policies, and tenant protections, and stated she believes it is time to step back from the role.
briefs.action_required: Note that a Council seat will be open in the October 2026 Burnaby municipal election.

key_dates.label: Burnaby municipal election — Councillor Gu's seat becomes open

briefs.industries: Professional Services, Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to Recognize Joanne Morgan as Burnaby's 2025 Outstanding Citizen of the Year
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has voted unanimously to approve Joanne Morgan as the 2025 Outstanding Citizen of the Year, with the Kushiro Cup to be presented at the City Appreciation Dinner on May 1, 2026. This is a civic honours decision and does not affect business regulations or operations.

key_dates.label: Kushiro Cup presentation at Burnaby City Appreciation Dinner

briefs.industries: Professional Services
